mechanism_narrative: A falling FEDFUNDS rate over the prior quarter signals easier discount conditions and supports equity multiples, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days. The signal is strongest when inflation is not accelerating sharply.
failure_condition: Reject if 20-day forward SPX returns are not positive on average when FEDFUNDS has declined over the prior 3 months, or if the signal turns negative during easing periods in a rolling 2-year test.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid rise in UNRATE above its recent trend signals labor-market deterioration and weaker household income growth, which should lower S&P 500 returns over the next month as earnings expectations are revised down.
failure_condition: Reject if triggered observations are followed by non-negative 20-day SPX returns more than 60% of the time, or if the average forward return after the trigger is not below the unconditional mean.

mechanism_narrative: When RSAFS accelerates while HOUST remains weak, it indicates consumption is holding up but housing demand is soft, a mixed-demand late-cycle setup that compresses equity returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if the joint condition of rising RSAFS and subdued HOUST does not predict lower 60-day SPX returns than either proxy alone, or if the interaction term is not negative in rolling out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: Rising INDPRO after a prior slowdown signals manufacturing re-acceleration and improving earnings breadth, which should lift S&P 500 returns over the next week when production is moving above trend.
failure_condition: Reject if weekly-triggered instances of rising INDPRO do not produce positive 5-day SPX returns more often than not, or if the effect vanishes after one month of out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: A high and rising FEDFUNDS rate combined with elevated CPIAUCSL growth signals restrictive real policy, which should compress valuations and generate negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS > 4.5% and CPIAUCSL inflation trend rising
failure_condition: Reject if the combined high-rate/high-inflation trigger is followed by positive 20-day SPX returns in a majority of cases, or if the interaction effect is not significantly negative versus single-series baselines.

mechanism_narrative: Stable PAYEMS with falling UNRATE indicates broad employment gains and resilient household demand, which should support positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter as earnings outlooks improve.
failure_condition: Reject if periods with rising payrolls and declining unemployment fail to beat the unconditional 60-day SPX return, or if the sign flips negative in a rolling 3-year sample.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp slowdown in GDPC1 growth marks demand deceleration and weaker aggregate revenue prospects, which should lead to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days once the slowdown is visible in the quarterly data.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 quarterly growth falls below 1% annualized
failure_condition: Reject if low-growth GDPC1 quarters are not followed by below-median 60-day SPX returns, or if the relationship is not negative in post-publication walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: A housing rebound signaled by rising HOUST alongside stronger RSAFS indicates improving domestic demand and should raise S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days as cyclical revenue expectations improve.
failure_condition: Reject if consecutive housing upturns with firm retail sales do not precede positive 20-day SPX returns more often than baseline, or if the interaction term is not positive in out-of-sample tests.
